_how_to_add_data.description: TO ADD MORE DATA IN FUTURE — ZERO CODE CHANGES NEEDED. Edit this JSON file only.

add_new_drug.step_1: Copy any existing entry from the 'drugs' array
add_new_drug.step_2: Assign next available drug_id (e.g. if last is DR026, use DR027)
add_new_drug.step_3: Set 'target_pathway' to one of the exact keys in 'pathway_synonym_map'
add_new_drug.step_4: Set 'mechanism_action' to one of: Inhibitor | Activator | Enhancer | Replacement | Antagonist | Blocker | Degrader
add_new_drug.step_5: Add the new drug_id to 'pathway_drug_index' under the correct pathway key
add_new_drug.step_6: Save file — app picks it up automatically on next restart
add_new_drug.warning: Safety fields must come from FDA DailyMed only. Never guess black_box_warning or pediatric_note.

add_new_pathway.step_1: Add a new key + synonyms array to 'pathway_synonym_map'
add_new_pathway.step_2: Add the new pathway key + empty list to 'pathway_drug_index'
add_new_pathway.step_3: Tag relevant drugs with the new pathway in their 'target_pathway' field
add_new_pathway.warning: Pathway key must be identical across pathway_synonym_map, pathway_drug_index, and drug entries

add_new_synonym.step_2: Add synonym string to its array
add_new_synonym.note: No other changes needed. Matching engine reads this automatically.

pediatric_flag_values.GREEN: Well-tolerated in children, approved or widely used pediatrically
pediatric_flag_values.YELLOW: Use with caution — monitoring required or limited pediatric data
pediatric_flag_values.RED: Avoid in children — not approved, serious pediatric-specific risk, or absolute contraindication

pathway_synonym_map._purpose: Maps canonical pathway names to all synonyms the LLM might return. Add synonyms here — no code change needed.

pathway_drug_index._purpose: Quick lookup: pathway key → list of drug_ids that target it. Update when adding drugs — no code change needed.

mechanism_action_rules._purpose: Rules engine uses this to filter drugs by GoF/LoF. Add new rules here — no code change needed.

Gain of Function.rationale: GoF = pathway too loud. Need to turn it DOWN.

Loss of Function.rationale: LoF = pathway too quiet OR negative regulator lost. Need to compensate or inhibit what is overactive downstream.
Loss of Function.note: LoF of a negative regulator (NF1, SYNGAP1) causes downstream overactivation — Inhibitors ARE valid for the overactive downstream pathway.

Dominant Negative.rationale: DN = broken protein sabotages healthy proteins. Suppress the bad pathway.

drugs.pathway_action: Allosteric inhibition of mTORC1 — reduces phosphorylation of S6K1 and 4EBP1, suppressing lymphocyte proliferation and cell growth.
drugs.evidence_summary: Multiple clinical studies show >90% reduction in lymphoproliferation in ALPS. FDA-approved for LAM. Strong preclinical data across mTOR-driven conditions.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for transplant rejection and LAM. Off-label for ALPS, TSC tumors.

safety.black_box_warning: Increased risk of serious infections. Risk of lymphoma and other malignancies. Increased mortality in non-renal transplant patients.
safety.pediatric_note: Not FDA-approved for transplant in patients under 13. Growth suppression with long-term use. Intense monitoring required.
safety.liver_toxicity: Moderate — monitor LFTs baseline and every 3 months
safety.missing_data: Long-term impact on puberty and bone development unknown. No RCT data in children under 5.

drugs.validation_plan: Phase II open-label trial. S6K phosphorylation as target engagement biomarker. Lymphocyte apoptosis assay as pharmacodynamic endpoint.

drugs.pathway_action: Second-generation mTOR inhibitor. Same mTORC1 target as Sirolimus. Broader FDA approvals including pediatric TSC.
drugs.evidence_summary: EXIST-1 and EXIST-2 trials: significant tumor reduction in TSC. FDA-approved for TSC-related SEGA and renal AML.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for TSC (SEGA, renal AML), RCC, PNET, breast cancer.

safety.black_box_warning: Non-infectious pneumonitis. Serious and fatal infections. Renal failure. Abnormal wound healing.
safety.pediatric_note: FDA-approved for TSC in pediatric patients ≥1 year. Stomatitis most common side effect. Growth monitoring required.
safety.missing_data: Optimal treatment duration not established. Rebound tumor growth on discontinuation reported.

drugs.validation_plan: Established — EXIST trial extensions ongoing. MRI volumetrics as primary endpoint.

drugs.pathway_action: Activates AMPK which indirectly suppresses mTORC1 via energy sensing. Gentler, indirect mTOR dampening — weaker than direct inhibitors.
drugs.evidence_summary: Preclinical data in TSC and overgrowth models. No significant RCT data in rare disease indications yet.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for Type 2 Diabetes. Off-label investigational for rare mTOR-driven diseases.

safety.black_box_warning: Lactic acidosis — rare but fatal. Risk increased with renal impairment, hepatic impairment, excessive alcohol.
safety.pediatric_note: FDA-approved for type 2 diabetes in children ≥10 years. Lactic acidosis risk in young children with renal impairment.
safety.liver_toxicity: Low — contraindicated in hepatic impairment due to lactic acidosis risk
safety.missing_data: mTOR inhibition at standard doses may be insufficient for rare disease indications.

drugs.validation_plan: Pilot study: measure S6K phosphorylation in patients before considering disease-specific trial.

drugs.pathway_action: Highly selective inhibitor of PIK3CA (PI3Kα isoform) — directly targets the mutant kinase responsible for PI3K/AKT hyperactivation in PROS.
drugs.evidence_summary: EPIK-P1 trial: significant overgrowth lesion volume reduction. FDA-approved for PROS 2022.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for PROS (2022) and PIK3CA-mutant breast cancer.

safety.black_box_warning: Severe hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis. Severe cutaneous adverse reactions (SCARs) including SJS and TEN.
safety.pediatric_note: Approved for adults and pediatric patients ≥2 years for PROS. Hyperglycemia is primary toxicity — mandatory diabetes monitoring protocol.
safety.missing_data: Long-term effects on insulin sensitivity in growing children not fully characterized.

drugs.validation_plan: Established — EPIK-P1 extension. Monitor HbA1c quarterly. MRI volumetrics at 6 months.

drugs.pathway_action: Targets PI3K delta isoform (immune-cell specific) — MECHANISTIC MISMATCH for somatic PIK3CA-alpha mutations in overgrowth syndromes.
drugs.evidence_summary: Approved for CLL. PI3Kδ is immune-cell specific — wrong isoform for PROS. Included as safety filter test case.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for CLL and relapsed follicular lymphoma. Wrong isoform for PROS.

safety.black_box_warning: Fatal hepatotoxicity. Fatal diarrhea or colitis. Fatal pneumonitis. Fatal and serious infections.
safety.pediatric_note: Not approved in children. Four black box warnings. Severe hepatotoxicity particularly dangerous in pediatric patients.
safety.liver_toxicity: SEVERE — Black Box Warning for fatal hepatotoxicity
safety.missing_data: Wrong isoform for PI3Kα diseases — high toxicity with no mechanistic rationale.

drugs.validation_plan: BLOCKED — mechanistic mismatch. Safety filter flags RED. Do not use for PIK3CA-alpha diseases.

drugs.pathway_action: Targets mTOR downstream of PI3K/AKT — less targeted than direct PI3K inhibition but available alternative when Alpelisib not tolerated.
drugs.evidence_summary: Case reports in PIK3CA-ROS show partial benefit. Less effective than Alpelisib but widely available.
drugs.approval_status: Off-label for PIK3CA-ROS. FDA-approved for TSC.

safety.black_box_warning: Non-infectious pneumonitis. Serious infections. Renal failure.
safety.pediatric_note: Approved ≥1 year for TSC indications.
safety.missing_data: Head-to-head vs Alpelisib for PI3K/AKT diseases lacking.

drugs.validation_plan: Second-line to Alpelisib. MRI volumetrics at 6 months.

drugs.pathway_action: Selective MEK1/2 inhibitor — blocks downstream RAS/MAPK signaling regardless of upstream cause (NF1 LoF, PTPN11 GoF, SYNGAP1 LoF of negative regulator).
drugs.evidence_summary: SPRINT trial: 66% of pediatric NF1 patients with inoperable neurofibromas had ≥20% tumor reduction. Best pediatric safety profile among MEK inhibitors.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for NF1 pediatric patients ≥2 years with symptomatic plexiform neurofibromas.

safety.black_box_warning: None.
safety.pediatric_note: FDA-approved pediatric indication ≥2 years. GI toxicity and rash are primary concerns. CK monitoring required.
safety.missing_data: Long-term tumor regrowth on discontinuation. Optimal treatment duration unknown.

drugs.validation_plan: Established — SPRINT extension. MRI volumetrics and patient-reported outcomes.

drugs.pathway_action: Potent MEK1/2 inhibitor. Same RAS/MAPK target as Selumetinib — higher potency but significantly worse cardiac and pediatric safety profile.
drugs.evidence_summary: Emerging trial data in NF1-glioma and Noonan HCM. More potent than Selumetinib but not preferred pediatric option.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for BRAF V600E melanoma. Off-label for NF1, Noonan.

safety.black_box_warning: None specific, but serious: cardiomyopathy, retinal vein occlusion, interstitial lung disease, serious hemorrhage.
safety.pediatric_note: Not FDA-approved for pediatric RAS/MAPK indications. Cardiac monitoring essential. More toxic than Selumetinib in children.
safety.missing_data: Long-term cardiac safety in Noonan HCM. Growth impact with chronic MEK inhibition in children.

drugs.validation_plan: Reserve for NF1-glioma adults. Noonan HCM trial requires serial echocardiography as primary endpoint.

drugs.pathway_action: Reduces RAS membrane prenylation (farnesylation) — indirectly dampens RAS/MAPK overactivation. Weaker than direct MEK inhibition but better safety profile.
drugs.evidence_summary: SYNGAP1 mouse models: synaptic plasticity rescue. NF1 Phase II trial in children: safe but no significant cognitive improvement.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for hypercholesterolemia. Off-label investigational for RASopathies.

safety.black_box_warning: None.
safety.pediatric_note: FDA-approved for familial hypercholesterolemia in children ≥10 years. Myopathy risk especially with CYP3A4 inhibitors.
safety.missing_data: CNS penetrance at therapeutic doses unclear. No SYNGAP1-specific human trial data.

drugs.validation_plan: Phase II RCT in SYNGAP1 patients ≥10 years. Vineland adaptive scales as primary endpoint. ERK phosphorylation in PBMCs as biomarker.

drugs.pathway_action: Addresses secondary mTOR overactivation downstream of RAS/MAPK hyperactivation in RASopathies — less targeted than MEK inhibition.
drugs.evidence_summary: Preclinical evidence in RASopathy models. Downstream of the primary defect — less targeted than MEK inhibitors.
drugs.approval_status: Off-label for RASopathy neuro indications.

safety.black_box_warning: Increased risk of serious infections. Malignancy risk.
safety.pediatric_note: Neurodevelopmental safety of chronic mTOR inhibition in young children is unknown and concerning.
safety.missing_data: CNS safety of mTOR inhibition in neurodevelopment unknown. Optimal duration unclear.

drugs.validation_plan: iPSC-derived neuron model first. Measure spine density and LTP rescue before any human trial.

drugs.pathway_action: Inhibits IMDH — blocks purine synthesis in lymphocytes, indirectly suppressing autoreactive lymphocyte proliferation in ALPS.
drugs.evidence_summary: Second-line agent in ALPS when Sirolimus not tolerated. Reduces lymphoproliferation indirectly.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for organ transplant. Off-label for ALPS.

safety.black_box_warning: Embryofetal toxicity. Increased risk of lymphoma and serious infections.
safety.pediatric_note: Used in pediatric transplant patients with dose adjustment by body surface area.
safety.missing_data: Optimal dosing in ALPS children under 6 not established.

drugs.validation_plan: Registry analysis of ALPS patients on MMF. Compare lymphocyte biomarkers pre/post treatment.

drugs.pathway_action: B-cell depletion via CD20 targeting — reduces autoantibody production in ALPS autoimmune manifestations. Addresses symptoms not root FAS mechanism.
drugs.evidence_summary: Effective for autoimmune cytopenias in ALPS. Does not address lymphoproliferation or root FAS defect.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for NHL, CLL, RA. Off-label for ALPS autoimmune cytopenias.

safety.black_box_warning: Fatal infusion reactions. Severe mucocutaneous reactions. Hepatitis B reactivation. Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy (PML).
safety.pediatric_note: Not FDA-approved for pediatric use. PML risk in immunocompromised children is particularly concerning.
safety.liver_toxicity: Risk of hepatitis B reactivation — screen HBsAg and anti-HBc before use
safety.missing_data: Long-term immune reconstitution data in pediatric ALPS lacking.

drugs.validation_plan: Limit to ALPS refractory autoimmune cytopenia only. Monitor IgG levels as safety biomarker.

drugs.pathway_action: Modulates lysosomal function and innate immune signaling — mild immunomodulation for ALPS adjunct therapy.
drugs.evidence_summary: Limited case reports suggest benefit in mild ALPS. Used adjunctively, not as monotherapy.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for lupus, malaria, RA. Off-label for ALPS.

safety.black_box_warning: None.
safety.pediatric_note: Well-tolerated in children. Used in pediatric lupus. Annual ophthalmology exam required for retinopathy monitoring.
safety.missing_data: No prospective ALPS pediatric trial data.

drugs.validation_plan: N-of-1 trial in mild ALPS. Endpoint: reduction in double-negative T-cells (DNT biomarker).

drugs.pathway_action: Multi-target: enhances GABAergic tone, modulates TRPV1, antagonizes GPR55, and modulates voltage-gated sodium channels. FDA-approved for Dravet and TSC seizures.
drugs.evidence_summary: GWPCARE1: 39% reduction in convulsive seizures in Dravet. CARE-E: ~49% reduction in TSC seizures. Both FDA-approved.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for Dravet Syndrome, LGS, and TSC seizures ≥2 years.

safety.black_box_warning: Hepatotoxicity — especially with concurrent valproate use.
safety.pediatric_note: Approved ≥2 years. LFT monitoring mandatory especially with other AEDs.
safety.liver_toxicity: HIGH — primary safety concern with AED polypharmacy
safety.missing_data: Long-term hepatic safety in polypharmacy children unclear.

drugs.validation_plan: Established — GWPCARE and CARE-E extension studies ongoing.

drugs.pathway_action: Positive allosteric modulator of GABA-A receptors — enhances inhibitory neurotransmission to compensate for excitatory/inhibitory imbalance.
drugs.evidence_summary: FDA-approved adjunctive therapy for LGS. Widely used off-label in Dravet syndrome.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for LGS ≥2 years. Off-label for Dravet.

safety.black_box_warning: Concomitant use with opioids may cause profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, and death.
safety.pediatric_note: Approved ≥2 years for LGS. Sedation and behavioral effects notable. Physical dependence risk.
safety.missing_data: Optimal duration before tolerance develops unclear.

drugs.validation_plan: Adjunctive use only. Neuropsychological testing at 6 months to monitor behavioral effects.

drugs.pathway_action: Releases serotonin and activates sigma-1 receptors — modulates neuronal excitability via serotonergic pathway. FDA-approved specifically for Dravet.
drugs.evidence_summary: Pivotal trial: 62% of patients had >50% seizure reduction. FDA-approved 2020 for Dravet.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for Dravet Syndrome and LGS ≥2 years under REMS.

safety.black_box_warning: REMS REQUIRED. Cardiac valvulopathy and pulmonary arterial hypertension risk.
safety.pediatric_note: Approved ≥2 years under mandatory REMS. Echocardiogram every 6 months required.
safety.missing_data: Very long-term cardiac outcomes in children receiving multi-year treatment.

drugs.validation_plan: Established — mandatory REMS echocardiography program. Annual cardiac imaging.

drugs.pathway_action: Multiple mechanisms including GABA enhancement and sodium channel modulation. First-line for Dravet but significant toxicity burden.
drugs.evidence_summary: First-line AED for Dravet. Partial seizure control in majority of patients. High toxicity, especially in young children.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for seizures, bipolar, migraine.

safety.black_box_warning: Hepatotoxicity especially in children <2 years — can be fatal. Teratogenicity — major congenital malformations. Pancreatitis — can be fatal.
safety.pediatric_note: SEVERE hepatotoxicity risk in children under 2 years. Fatal cases reported. Risk highest with polypharmacy.
safety.liver_toxicity: SEVERE in infants — Black Box Warning
safety.missing_data: CBD co-administration raises hepatotoxicity risk — mandatory LFT monitoring.

drugs.validation_plan: If used, mandatory LFT monitoring every 3 months. Avoid entirely in children under 2 years.

drugs.pathway_action: ABSOLUTE CONTRAINDICATION IN SCN1A LoF — blocks sodium channels, worsening Nav1.1 deficiency and precipitating seizure storms in Dravet.
drugs.evidence_summary: Known to WORSEN Dravet syndrome. Included as safety filter test case — system must catch and block this automatically.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for seizures. CONTRAINDICATED in Dravet/SCN1A LoF.

safety.black_box_warning: Aplastic anemia and agranulocytosis. Serious dermatologic reactions including SJS and TEN.
safety.pediatric_note: Can precipitate seizure storms and status epilepticus in Dravet — potentially fatal. NEVER use in SCN1A LoF patients.
safety.missing_data: N/A — do not use in Dravet.

drugs.validation_plan: BLOCKED. Safety rule: sodium channel blocker + SCN1A LoF = absolute contraindication.

drugs.pathway_action: Sodium channel blocker — same mechanistic concern as Carbamazepine in SCN1A LoF. Inconsistent results in Dravet, generally avoided.
drugs.evidence_summary: Controversial in Dravet — some seizure worsening reports, others partial benefit. Generally avoided.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for epilepsy and bipolar. Caution/avoid in Dravet.

safety.black_box_warning: Serious skin reactions including SJS and TEN — risk highest in children and with rapid titration.
safety.pediatric_note: SJS risk highest in children. Must titrate extremely slowly. Avoid in Dravet.
safety.missing_data: Inconsistent data in Dravet — predictors of worsening unknown.

drugs.validation_plan: Avoid in Dravet unless all other options exhausted. Use only with extreme caution and slow titration.

drugs.pathway_action: Blocks C5 cleavage — prevents terminal complement activation (MAC formation) regardless of which upstream complement gene is mutated.
drugs.evidence_summary: Transformed aHUS from near-universal ESRD to manageable disease. >80% achieve TMA remission.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for aHUS and PNH.

safety.black_box_warning: LIFE-THREATENING AND FATAL MENINGOCOCCAL INFECTIONS. Vaccination against N. meningitidis REQUIRED at least 2 weeks before treatment.
safety.pediatric_note: Approved for pediatric use including infants. Meningococcal vaccination and prophylactic antibiotics mandatory.
safety.missing_data: Optimal duration unclear — lifelong vs discontinuation protocols debated.

drugs.validation_plan: Established. Monitor CH50 and sC5b-9. Meningococcal vaccine mandatory before first dose.

drugs.pathway_action: Same C5 blocking mechanism as Eculizumab. Engineered half-life extension enables 8-week dosing vs every 2 weeks for Eculizumab.
drugs.evidence_summary: Non-inferior to Eculizumab. Now preferred over Eculizumab due to 8-week dosing convenience.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for aHUS and PNH.

safety.black_box_warning: MENINGOCOCCAL INFECTIONS — same as Eculizumab. Vaccination required.
safety.pediatric_note: Approved for pediatric use. Same vaccination requirements as Eculizumab.
safety.missing_data: Long-term comparative data vs Eculizumab in pediatric aHUS limited.

drugs.validation_plan: Preferred over Eculizumab where available. Monitor renal function and complement markers.

drugs.pathway_action: Irreversibly inhibits GABA transaminase — increases synaptic GABA levels by blocking its breakdown. First-line for TSC-associated infantile spasms.
drugs.evidence_summary: First-line for infantile spasms in TSC. Does not affect tumor burden.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for infantile spasms (1 month to 2 years) and refractory CPS.

safety.black_box_warning: PERMANENT BILATERAL VISION LOSS — occurs in up to 1/3 of patients.
safety.pediatric_note: FDA-approved for infantile spasms in infants 1 month to 2 years. Permanent vision loss is devastating in developing children.
major_side_effects: Permanent vision loss (up to 1/3 of patients)
safety.missing_data: Optimal duration before vision loss risk becomes prohibitive unclear.

drugs.validation_plan: Visual field testing every 3 months mandatory. Discontinue immediately if vision changes.

drugs.pathway_action: FDA-approved for Noonan short stature. Compensates for growth impairment — does not address RAS/MAPK overactivation directly.
drugs.evidence_summary: FDA-approved for Noonan short stature. Does not address cardiac or tumor manifestations.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for Noonan-associated short stature.

safety.black_box_warning: None.
safety.pediatric_note: Generally safe in children. Theoretical tumor promotion concern in RASopathy context.
safety.missing_data: Theoretical tumor promotion risk with GH in RASopathy context not well quantified.

drugs.validation_plan: Established. Post-marketing surveillance for tumor incidence in Noonan GH recipients.

drugs.pathway_action: Blocks excessive NMDA receptor activation — dampens glutamatergic overactivation from SYNGAP1 LoF-driven excitatory/inhibitory imbalance.
drugs.evidence_summary: SYNGAP1 mouse models show improved social behavior with NMDA antagonism. Used off-label in pediatric autism.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for Alzheimer's in adults. Off-label in pediatric neurodevelopmental disorders.

safety.black_box_warning: None.
safety.pediatric_note: Not FDA-approved in children. Small autism trials show generally acceptable tolerability.
safety.missing_data: No SYNGAP1-specific human data. Optimal dose for synaptic target engagement in children unknown.

drugs.validation_plan: N-of-1 crossover trial. EEG gamma oscillation power as proxy biomarker for synaptic excitation.

drugs.pathway_action: Recombinant human alpha-L-iduronidase — directly replaces the deficient enzyme in MPS-I (Hurler syndrome), addressing root LoF mechanism.
drugs.evidence_summary: FDA-approved ERT for MPS-I. Reduces urinary GAGs and improves respiratory function. Does not cross BBB.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for MPS-I.

safety.black_box_warning: Anaphylaxis and severe allergic reactions during infusion — emergency equipment required.
safety.pediatric_note: Approved for pediatric use including infants. Infusion reactions more common with first doses. Pre-medicate.
safety.missing_data: Does not penetrate blood-brain barrier — CNS manifestations not addressed by IV ERT.

drugs.validation_plan: Established. Pre-medicate with antihistamines. Monitor anti-laronidase antibody titers.

drugs.pathway_action: Inhibits MMP-9 and microglial activation — may partially compensate for UBE3A LoF via synaptic remodeling in Angelman syndrome.
drugs.evidence_summary: Angelman mouse model showed improved synaptic function. Small human trial showed modest improvement.
drugs.approval_status: FDA-approved for bacterial infections. Off-label investigational for Angelman.

safety.black_box_warning: None.
safety.pediatric_note: AVOID IN CHILDREN UNDER 8 YEARS — causes permanent dental staining and tooth enamel hypoplasia. Most Angelman patients diagnosed in early childhood.
safety.missing_data: Mechanism of benefit in Angelman unclear. Dental staining renders inappropriate for most Angelman patients.

drugs.validation_plan: Only consider in patients over 8 years. Low priority agent for Angelman.
